scope.summary:
Goal: Wire per-surface context menus for six additional surfaces using the `context_menu` infra from MT-019 and the dispatch pattern established in MT-020. The six surfaces are: (1) Loom graph node, (2) Canvas board node/card, (3) source-control change row, (4) console/list rows (debug console + generic list), (5) drawer item (bottom stash shelf), (6) status bar segment.

Expected diff shape: Create six new files `src/frontend/context_menu/{loom_node_menu,canvas_node_menu,source_control_menu,console_row_menu,drawer_item_menu,status_bar_menu}.rs`. Register all six in `src/frontend/context_menu/mod.rs`. Edit each surface's existing widget file to add the secondary-click handler. Extend `handle_context_menu_action` (from MT-020) to cover the new action ids.

--- SURFACE 1: Loom graph node ---
React reference: `app/src/components/LoomBlockPanel.tsx` — shows block properties (title, pinned, favorite, tags). `app/src/lib/api.ts` lines: `getLoomBlock` (1611), `updateLoomBlock` (1890), `createLoomEdge` (1840), `setLoomBlockPinOrder` (1955).
Menu items:
  - `loom.open`              label="Open Block"
  - `loom.open_to_side`      label="Open to Side"
  - separator
  - `loom.rename`            label="Rename..."
  - `loom.pin`               label="Pin" / "Unpin"   toggles `LoomBlock.pinned`
  - `loom.favorite`          label="Favorite" / "Unfavorite"  toggles `LoomBlock.favorite`
  - separator
  - `loom.connect`           label="Connect to..."  (opens a connection picker — stub in V1 with a toast)
  - `loom.disconnect`        label="Disconnect"      enabled=if node has edges
  - separator
  - `loom.copy_block_id`     label="Copy Block ID"
  - `loom.reveal_in_panel`   label="Reveal in Block Panel"
  - separator
  - `loom.delete`            label="Delete Block"   (stub — requires confirmation dialog, same pattern as explorer.delete)
Right-click trigger: secondary click on a Loom graph node widget in the graph surface. The graph surface widget is part of `src/frontend/` (a net-new native surface replacing `app/src/components/LoomSearchV2Panel.tsx` / `LoomBlockPanel.tsx` as a thin placeholder). Attach right-click to each rendered node's `egui::Response`.
Action handlers:
  - `loom.open`: open block in new tab via `PaneRegistry::open_tab(content_type=LoomBlock, content_id=block_id)`.
  - `loom.open_to_side`: split right then open.
  - `loom.rename`: inline edit same as `explorer.rename` in MT-020; calls `updateLoomBlock(workspaceId, blockId, {title: new_name})`.
  - `loom.pin` / `loom.favorite`: call `updateLoomBlock(workspaceId, blockId, {pinned: bool})` / `{favorite: bool}`.
  - `loom.copy_block_id`: `ctx.copy_text(block.block_id)`.
  - `loom.reveal_in_panel`: open / focus `LoomBlockPanel` pane for the block.
  - `loom.connect` / `loom.disconnect` / `loom.delete`: stub with `tracing::warn!`.
  - `loom.disconnect` enable-check: call `queryLoomView` or inspect the loaded edge list for edges connected to this node.

--- SURFACE 2: Canvas board node/card ---
React reference: `app/src/components/LoomCanvasBoard.tsx` — renders placements (`LoomCanvasPlacement`), handles drag, edge creation, card creation. Backend APIs: `updateCanvasPlacement` (1749), `removeCanvasPlacement` (1781), `createLoomEdge` (1840), `addCanvasVisualEdge` (1793), `removeCanvasVisualEdge` (1813), `getLoomBlock` (1611).
Menu items:
  - `canvas.open_block`      label="Open Block"     enabled=if placement has a block_id (not a card)
  - `canvas.edit_card`       label="Edit Card Text"  enabled=if placement is a card (text-only)
  - separator
  - `canvas.connect_to`      label="Connect to..."  (semantic edge — stub V1)
  - `canvas.add_visual_edge` label="Add Visual Link" (visual-only edge — stub V1)
  - `canvas.remove_edges`    label="Remove All Connections" enabled=if node has edges
  - separator
  - `canvas.move_to_front`   label="Bring to Front"
  - `canvas.move_to_back`    label="Send to Back"
  - separator
  - `canvas.copy_block_id`   label="Copy Block ID"  enabled=if has block_id
  - separator
  - `canvas.remove`          label="Remove from Canvas" (removes placement, NOT the block)
  - `canvas.delete_block`    label="Delete Block"   enabled=if has block_id  (stub)
Right-click trigger: secondary click on a canvas placement widget (the card div / block preview div rendered in `LoomCanvasBoard` equivalent in `src/frontend/`). Pass `placement_id` and `placement_kind` (block vs. card) into the menu builder.
Action handlers:
  - `canvas.open_block`: open block in tab.
  - `canvas.edit_card`: enter inline text-edit mode on the canvas card (egui `TextEdit` in place).
  - `canvas.move_to_front` / `canvas.move_to_back`: call `updateCanvasPlacement(workspaceId, canvasBlockId, placementId, {z_order: max+1})` / `{z_order: 0}`. Backend: `PATCH /api/workspaces/{ws}/loom/canvas/{canvas_block_id}/placements/{placement_id}`.
  - `canvas.remove`: call `removeCanvasPlacement(workspaceId, canvasBlockId, placementId)`. Backend: `DELETE /api/workspaces/{ws}/loom/canvas/{canvas_block_id}/placements/{placement_id}`.
  - `canvas.remove_edges`: call `removeCanvasVisualEdge` for all edges connected to this placement. Backend: `DELETE /api/workspaces/{ws}/loom/canvas/{canvas_block_id}/visual_edges/{edge_id}`.
  - Stubs: `canvas.connect_to`, `canvas.add_visual_edge`, `canvas.delete_block` — `tracing::warn!`.

--- SURFACE 3: Source-control change row ---
React reference: `app/src/components/SourceControlPanel.tsx` — the change list renders each `SourceControlStatusEntry` as a button (lines 457-481). The panel exposes `stageSourceControlPaths` (2068), `unstageSourceControlPaths` (2078), `discardSourceControlPaths` (2088), `getSourceControlDiff` (2057), `getSourceControlBlame` (2143), `commitSourceControl` (2099). In the native shell these same backend REST calls are used.
Menu items:
  - `scm.stage`              label="Stage"          enabled=if entry has worktree changes
  - `scm.unstage`            label="Unstage"        enabled=if entry has index changes
  - `scm.discard`            label="Discard Changes" enabled=if entry has worktree changes  (requires confirm — stub confirm with `tracing::warn` in V1)
  - separator
  - `scm.diff_worktree`      label="Show Worktree Diff"
  - `scm.diff_staged`        label="Show Staged Diff"
  - `scm.blame`              label="Show Line Blame"
  - separator
  - `scm.commit_this_file`   label="Commit This File..." (opens commit message input for single-file commit — stub V1)
  - `scm.copy_path`          label="Copy Path"
Right-click trigger: secondary click on a source-control change row (`SourceControlStatusEntry`) in the native source-control panel (replaces `SourceControlPanel.tsx`).
Action handlers:
  - `scm.stage`: call `POST /api/source-control/stage` with `{repo_path, paths: [entry.path]}`. Maps to `stageSourceControlPaths(repoPath, [selectedPath])`.
  - `scm.unstage`: call `POST /api/source-control/unstage` with `{repo_path, paths: [entry.path]}`.
  - `scm.discard`: call `POST /api/source-control/discard` with `{repo_path, paths: [entry.path], force: true}` — ONLY after a confirm step. V1: stub with `tracing::warn!("discard: confirm dialog not yet implemented")`.
  - `scm.diff_worktree` / `scm.diff_staged`: call `GET /api/source-control/diff?repo_path={}&path={}&scope={worktree|staged}`. Maps to `getSourceControlDiff(repoPath, path, scope)`. Open the diff in a new tab or update the focused diff pane.
  - `scm.blame`: call `GET /api/source-control/blame?repo_path={}&path={}`. Maps to `getSourceControlBlame(repoPath, path)`. Display blame in the detail area.
  - `scm.copy_path`: `ctx.copy_text(entry.path)`.
  - `scm.commit_this_file`: stub (`tracing::warn!`).
Enable logic: read `SourceControlStatusEntry.index` and `SourceControlStatusEntry.worktree` flags to determine enabled state for stage/unstage/discard.

--- SURFACE 4: Console/list rows (debug console + generic list) ---
React reference: `app/src/components/DebugConsole.tsx` — renders `DebugConsoleEntry` list as `<ol>` with entries of kind input/result/error/output (lines 38-52). The generic list pattern is also used in `ProblemsView.tsx` and `TimelineView.tsx`.
Menu items for debug console rows:
  - `console.copy_line`      label="Copy Line"
  - `console.copy_all`       label="Copy All"
  - separator
  - `console.filter_kind`    label="Filter by Kind"  kind=Submenu with children:
      - `console.filter.input`  label="Input only"
      - `console.filter.result` label="Results only"
      - `console.filter.error`  label="Errors only"
      - `console.filter.output` label="Output only"
      - `console.filter.all`    label="Show All"
  - separator
  - `console.clear`          label="Clear Console"
Menu items for generic list rows (problems, timeline, job list, log tail):
  - `list.copy_text`         label="Copy"
  - `list.copy_all`          label="Copy All"
  - separator
  - `list.go_to_source`      label="Go to Source"    enabled=if entry has a file/line reference
  - `list.filter_kind`       label="Filter..."        kind=Submenu (same filter pattern as console)
Right-click trigger: secondary click on any row in the debug console list, problems list, timeline, or generic scrollable list. Pass the row's text content and kind as context to the menu builder.
Action handlers:
  - `console.copy_line` / `list.copy_text`: `ctx.copy_text(entry.text)`.
  - `console.copy_all` / `list.copy_all`: join all entries' text, copy.
  - `console.clear`: clear `entries: Vec<DebugConsoleEntry>` in the console state.
  - `console.filter.{kind}`: set a filter flag in the console display state.
  - `list.go_to_source`: navigate to the file/line if the entry carries source location; stub if not.

--- SURFACE 5: Drawer item (bottom stash shelf) ---
React reference: `app/src/components/AiJobsDrawer.tsx` shows a minimal drawer pattern (lines 28-60). The bottom drawer stash shelf (MT-023, C6) will add typed cards. This MT authors the context menu for drawer items; the drawer itself is implemented in MT-023 but the menu is authored here for C5 completeness. If MT-023 is not yet merged, attach the menu to a placeholder drawer item widget.
Menu items:
  - `drawer.stow`            label="Stow"           (minimize card to icon in shelf)
  - `drawer.pin`             label="Pin" / "Unpin"  (keep card always visible)
  - `drawer.promote`         label="Promote to Pane" (open card content as a full pane)
  - `drawer.send_to_pane`    label="Send to Pane..."  kind=Submenu (list of open panes)
  - separator
  - `drawer.copy_to_prompt`  label="Copy to Prompt"  (paste card text into focused chat/editor)
  - `drawer.attach_evidence` label="Attach as Evidence" (stub — links to the Handshake evidence system)
  - `drawer.convert_artifact` label="Convert to Artifact..." (stub)
  - separator
  - `drawer.discard`         label="Discard"         (remove card from shelf)
Right-click trigger: secondary click on any drawer item card in the bottom stash shelf (MT-023 widget). If MT-023 is not merged, attach to a stubbed placeholder `DrawerItem` widget in `src/frontend/drawer/`.
Action handlers:
  - `drawer.stow` / `drawer.pin`: mutate drawer item state flags.
  - `drawer.promote`: call `PaneRegistry::open_tab` with the card's content.
  - `drawer.send_to_pane`: open submenu listing open panes by name; on selection, move the card content to that pane as a new tab.
  - `drawer.copy_to_prompt`: `ctx.copy_text(card.text_content)` + optionally signal the focused editor pane to paste.
  - `drawer.attach_evidence` / `drawer.convert_artifact` / `drawer.discard`: stub with `tracing::warn!`.

--- SURFACE 6: Status bar segment ---
React reference: `app/src/components/SystemStatus.tsx` — shows system status with a refresh button. The native status bar will have segments (backend health, branch, linter, line/col, encoding, etc.). No existing React context menu for status bar.
Menu items (shown when any status bar segment is right-clicked):
  - `statusbar.copy_segment` label="Copy"
  - separator
  - `statusbar.toggle_visibility` label="Hide" / "Show [segment name]"  (toggle segment visibility)
  - separator
  - `statusbar.open_panel`   label="Open [related panel]"  e.g. right-click health segment → "Open System Status"; right-click branch → "Open Source Control"
  - `statusbar.refresh`      label="Refresh"               (re-fetch the segment's data)
Right-click trigger: secondary click on a status bar segment in the bottom status bar widget (MT-011 or the shell root). Pass `segment_id: &'static str` and `segment_label: String` into the menu builder.
Action handlers:
  - `statusbar.copy_segment`: `ctx.copy_text(segment.display_text)`.
  - `statusbar.toggle_visibility`: mutate a `HashMap<segment_id, bool>` in the shell settings state.
  - `statusbar.open_panel`: open the relevant panel in a pane based on `segment_id` (e.g. `"health"` → open System Status pane, `"branch"` → open Source Control pane).
  - `statusbar.refresh`: call the segment's data refresh callback.

AccessKit obligations: same pattern as MT-020. Every right-click target in Part 2 must carry a stable `author_id`: `loom_node_{block_id}`, `canvas_node_{placement_id}`, `scm_row_{path_safe}`, `console_row_{index}`, `drawer_item_{item_id}`, `status_bar_{segment_id}`. Every menu item button carries `ctx_menu_item_{item.id}`.

Dependencies/unblock conditions:
- MT-019 (infra) and MT-020 (dispatch pattern) must be merged first.
- MT-022/MT-023 (C6 drawer) may not exist when this MT runs — stub gracefully.
- MT-006/MT-007/MT-008 (pane/split/pop-out) needed for `promote` and `send_to_pane` actions.

Risk if missed: Source-control, Loom graph, and canvas surfaces have no right-click affordances, which is the primary interaction model users expect. Swarm agents cannot target individual change rows, loom nodes, or canvas cards by right-click, making agent workflows dependent on keyboard shortcuts only.
acceptance_criteria: Six new files exist: `src/frontend/context_menu/{loom_node_menu,canvas_node_menu,source_control_menu,console_row_menu,drawer_item_menu,status_bar_menu}.rs`, all registered in `src/frontend/context_menu/mod.rs`.
`cargo build` (frontend crate) passes with no new errors.
Secondary-click on a Loom graph node opens a menu with Open Block, Rename, Pin/Unpin, Favorite, Copy Block ID — proven by egui_kittest or screenshot.
Secondary-click on a canvas placement opens a menu with Open Block (if has block_id), Remove from Canvas, Bring to Front, Send to Back — proven by egui_kittest or screenshot.
Secondary-click on a source-control change row opens a menu with Stage, Unstage, Show Worktree Diff, Show Staged Diff, Show Line Blame, Copy Path — proven by egui_kittest or screenshot.

Activating `scm.stage` on a row calls the backend `POST /api/source-control/stage` with the correct `{repo_path, paths}` payload — proven by integration test against real PG backend OR by asserting the HTTP request was constructed with the correct URL and body via a mock.
Activating `scm.diff_worktree` calls `GET /api/source-control/diff?scope=worktree` and the result is displayed in the diff area (proven by test asserting the diff state is updated).
Secondary-click on a debug console row opens a menu with Copy Line, Copy All, Filter by Kind (submenu with all five children), Clear Console — proven by egui_kittest or screenshot.
Activating `console.copy_line` copies the row's text to the egui clipboard (assert via `ctx.output(|o| o.copied_text)`).
Activating `console.clear` clears the entries list (assert that `entries.len() == 0` after action).
Secondary-click on a drawer item opens a menu with Stow, Pin/Unpin, Promote to Pane, Send to Pane, Copy to Prompt, Discard — proven by egui_kittest or screenshot.
Secondary-click on a status bar segment opens a menu with Copy, Hide, Open [related panel], Refresh — proven by egui_kittest or screenshot.
Activating `statusbar.open_panel` on the health segment opens the System Status pane (assert pane registry contains a pane with content_type=SystemStatus).
All item ids exactly match the stable ids in the scope — enforced by a unit test comparing constructed item lists against reference slices.
Every right-click surface target has a stable AccessKit author_id per the specification in scope.

`canvas.remove` calls `removeCanvasPlacement` and the placement is removed from the canvas state in the next frame (proven by integration test against real PG OR by asserting the API call was made with correct placement_id).
`loom.pin` toggles `LoomBlock.pinned` and calls `updateLoomBlock` — proven by test asserting the PATCH request body contains `{"pinned": true/false}`.
proof_targets: Run `cargo test -p handshake_frontend context_menu::source_control_menu -- --nocapture` and paste passing output, including the `scm.stage` API call assertion.
Run `cargo test -p handshake_frontend context_menu::canvas_node_menu -- --nocapture` and paste passing output, including the `canvas.remove` `removeCanvasPlacement` call assertion.
Run `cargo test -p handshake_frontend context_menu::console_row_menu -- --nocapture` and paste passing output for copy_line and clear.
egui_kittest or screenshot showing Loom node right-click menu with at least Open Block, Rename, Pin, Copy Block ID visible.
egui_kittest or screenshot showing source-control change row right-click menu with Stage, Unstage, Show Worktree Diff visible.
egui_kittest or screenshot showing status bar segment right-click menu.
Integration test (real PG): right-click stage a changed file → assert `SourceControlStatus.entries` for that path shows `index` set after the action. Paste passing test output.
implementation_notes: For `scm.stage/unstage/discard`, the native panel maintains a `selected_path: Option<String>` and `repo_path: String` in its widget state. Pass these into the menu builder and action handler.

Source-control diff display: when `scm.diff_worktree` or `scm.diff_staged` is activated, update a `DiffPaneState { patch: String, language: String }` stored in the pane's state (or in the source-control panel widget state). The diff panel widget reads this state and renders it. No Monaco — use a syntax-highlighted `egui::ScrollArea` with monospace font in V1 (WP-012 will add real diff rendering).

For `loom.pin` and `loom.favorite`, the current state must be known BEFORE the menu opens (to set the correct label). Read it from the already-loaded `LoomBlock` in the Loom graph surface state. Do NOT fetch from the backend on every right-click — use the cached block.

Canvas `move_to_front` / `move_to_back`: in V1, if `z_order` is not a field on `LoomCanvasPlacement`, fake it by tracking a local `Vec<PlacementId>` ordered list in the canvas surface state and reordering there. Persist only if `updateCanvasPlacement` supports a `z_order` field in the backend (check `app/src/lib/api.ts` line 1749 for the `UpdateCanvasPlacementInput` type). If not supported, stub with a local reorder and a TODO for backend support.

Drawer item `send_to_pane` submenu: build the submenu children dynamically at right-click time from `PaneRegistry::list_panes()`. Each child's `id` is `drawer.send_to_pane.{pane_id}` (dynamic ids are allowed for submenu children as long as the parent item's id `drawer.send_to_pane` is stable).

Status bar `open_panel` dispatch: use a lookup table `HashMap<&'static str, PaneContentType>` mapping segment ids to the panel to open. Example: `("health", PaneContentType::SystemStatus)`, `("branch", PaneContentType::SourceControl)`. This table lives in `status_bar_menu.rs`.

Console filter submenu: the filter state is stored in the console widget state as `filter: Option<DebugConsoleEntryKind>`. The `show_context_menu` return id triggers a mutation of this flag; the console list render applies the filter before iterating entries.

All async backend calls (stage, unstage, diff, blame, updateLoomBlock, removeCanvasPlacement) must be dispatched as tokio tasks (or equivalent async runtime) from the egui update thread, with results written back via a channel (`std::sync::mpsc` or `tokio::sync::mpsc`) that the widget polls each frame. Do NOT block the egui render thread.

Use `tracing::warn!` for all V1 stubs (connect, delete, discard confirm, commit_this_file, attach_evidence, convert_artifact, go_to_source with no location). Each stub must log the action id and the reason it is stubbed.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/LoomBlockPanel.tsx — reference for LoomBlock property fields (title, pinned, favorite, tags, block_id) and the updateLoomBlock call pattern.

app/src/components/LoomCanvasBoard.tsx — reference for canvas placement structure (placement_id, block_id, card text), edge kinds (semantic vs. visual), and the removeCanvasPlacement / addCanvasVisualEdge call pattern.
app/src/components/SourceControlPanel.tsx lines 254-378 — reference for stage/unstage/discard/blame/diff call pattern and the `SourceControlStatusEntry` shape (index/worktree flags).
app/src/components/DebugConsole.tsx lines 25-65 — reference for DebugConsoleEntry shape (kind: input|result|error|output, text) and the entries list rendering.
app/src/components/AiJobsDrawer.tsx lines 28-60 — reference for the drawer open/close toggle pattern and job list row structure.
app/src/components/SystemStatus.tsx — reference for health check data shape (status, db_status, message) used in the status bar segment.
binds_backend_api: POST /api/source-control/discard — `discardSourceControlPaths(repoPath, [path], force=true)` — used by `scm.discard` (stubbed in V1 pending confirm dialog).
GET /api/source-control/diff?repo_path=&path=&scope= — `getSourceControlDiff(repoPath, path, scope)` — used by `scm.diff_worktree` and `scm.diff_staged`.
PATCH /api/workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/blocks/{block_id} — `updateLoomBlock(workspaceId, blockId, {pinned, favorite, title})` — used by `loom.rename`, `loom.pin`, `loom.favorite`.

PATCH /api/workspaces/{workspace_id}/loom/canvas/{canvas_block_id}/placements/{placement_id} — `updateCanvasPlacement(workspaceId, canvasBlockId, placementId, patch)` — used by `canvas.move_to_front`, `canvas.move_to_back`.
GET /api/health — `getHealth()` — polled by the status bar health segment; `statusbar.refresh` triggers a re-poll.

risks: Loom node right-click menu uses stale cached block state (pinned=false) even after the block was pinned in a different pane, showing the wrong label and toggling in the wrong direction.
Canvas `remove_edges` calls `removeCanvasVisualEdge` for ALL edges including semantic Loom edges, silently destroying semantic graph relationships that are not canvas-local.
Source-control `scm.discard` is activated without a confirm step (V1 stub is `tracing::warn!`), but if the stub is accidentally removed or bypassed, it irreversibly destroys local changes.
Async backend calls from the egui render thread block the frame if not dispatched to a background task, causing visible freeze on slow network/DB.
Console `console.clear` clears the backend-sourced log entries permanently if the entries Vec is the authoritative store; the user loses debug history that cannot be recovered.
Drawer `send_to_pane` submenu builds pane ids dynamically; if pane ids change between the right-click and the submenu selection (pane closed), the action targets a non-existent pane.
Status bar `toggle_visibility` hides a segment but provides no way to restore it if the settings dialog (MT-018) does not expose segment visibility toggles.
Canvas `move_to_front` updates a local z-order list but does NOT persist it to the backend, causing z-order to reset on the next canvas load.

Multiple right-click handlers emit `request_context_menu` in the same frame (e.g. user right-clicks near the border between a canvas node and a source-control panel), triggering two menus with the last-write-wins invariant silently dropping one.
minimum_controls: Loom node pin/favorite: always re-read `block.pinned` / `block.favorite` from the freshest cached copy in the graph surface state when building the menu items list. After a successful `updateLoomBlock` call, update the local cache immediately from the API response.

Canvas `remove_edges`: check edge kind before deleting. Only call `removeCanvasVisualEdge` for visual-only edges (those in `canvas_board.visual_edges`). Do NOT call any semantic edge deletion API from this menu item. Add a comment explicitly documenting this constraint.

Discard stub: the `tracing::warn!` stub must include the string `STUB_NO_CONFIRM` in the log message so a grep can detect if it is ever accidentally replaced with a real call without the confirm dialog. Add a unit test asserting the returned action from `scm.discard` when `discard_confirmed=false` is `None` (no action taken).

Async backend calls: all calls must be dispatched via `tokio::task::spawn` (or equivalent) and must NEVER call `.await` on the egui update thread. Results arrive via a `std::sync::mpsc::channel` polled in the widget's `update()` call.

Console clear: document in the function's doc comment that this clears the IN-MEMORY display list only, not any persistent backend log. The backend log (if any) is unaffected. Add a unit test asserting that after `console.clear`, `entries.len() == 0`.

Drawer send_to_pane: snapshot the pane id at the moment the submenu child is activated. Before calling `PaneRegistry::open_tab`, verify the pane still exists; if not, open in the currently focused pane instead and log `tracing::warn!("target pane {pane_id} closed before send_to_pane completed")`.

Status bar visibility: if a segment is hidden via context menu, always ensure the settings dialog (MT-018) shows a 'Restore hidden status bar items' option. Document this dependency in a TODO comment pointing to MT-018.

Canvas z-order persistence: if `updateCanvasPlacement` does not support `z_order`, the local reorder MUST be documented as non-persistent with a `tracing::warn!("canvas z_order: local only, not persisted until backend supports z_order field")` on first use.

Multi-handler collision: enforce the MT-019 single-open invariant. Only one surface widget's secondary_click check will win because `request_context_menu` is last-write-wins and secondary_click events are consumed on use. Verify with a kittest that right-clicking a canvas node that overlaps a pane border opens exactly one menu.

wp_validator_review.reviewer: INTEGRATION_VALIDATOR(acting WP_VALIDATOR) session boa3epvo9 — operator-directed retro WP-Validator pass
wp_validator_review.method: deep code review + adversarial verify vs MT/WP/Master-Spec intent (governance paperwork non-blocking per operator)
wp_validator_review.one_line: Independently confirmed: all six per-surface context menus are behavior-complete with exact contract stable ids and honest enable/disable, and the real-resource backend claims (scm.stage POST, scm.diff GET scope=worktree, canvas.remove DELETE, canvas z_index PATCH, loom.pin/favorite PATCH {pinned}/{favorite}, remove_edges DELETE) are proven on the wire via a real in-process TcpListener against backend routes that genuinely EXIST and accept the exact bodies sent (flatten/Option deserialization verified) — not a self-authored mock.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC#1: Six new files under src/frontend/context_menu/ registered in mod.rs
acceptance_summary.evidence: All six surfaces (loom_ids/canvas_ids/scm_ids/console_ids+list_ids/drawer_ids/statusbar_ids) + builders + mappers + 5 net-new widget files (source_control.rs, loom_graph.rs, canvas_board.rs, debug_console.rs, drawer.rs) exist and are exported via lib.rs (commit 167c5fa0). Folded into context_menu_surfaces.rs + per-surface widget files rather than six files under context_menu/. Per operator directive this is file-layout paperwork, not a code gap — all six surfaces are present and behavior-complete.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC#2: cargo build (frontend crate) passes with no new errors
acceptance_summary.evidence: Ground truth established by validator: handshake_native crate + all test binaries compile clean; clippy --all-targets -D warnings GREEN.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC#3: Loom node right-click opens menu (Open Block/Rename/Pin/Favorite/Copy Block ID)
acceptance_summary.evidence: loom_node_context_items (context_menu_surfaces.rs:471) + loom_graph.rs widget; test_context_menu_surfaces_part2.rs::loom_node_right_click_opens_menu_and_dispatches asserts live ctx-menu.loom.{open,rename,pin,copy_block_id} MenuItem AccessKit nodes via egui_kittest secondary-click + Pin dispatch (SetPinned target:true).

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC#4: Canvas placement menu (Open Block if block_id, Remove, Bring to Front, Send to Back)
acceptance_summary.evidence: canvas_node_context_items kind-gated (open_block enabled only for Block); canvas_node_right_click_opens_menu_and_remove_dispatches asserts the four leaves live + Remove dispatch.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC#5: Source-control row menu (Stage/Unstage/Worktree+Staged Diff/Blame/Copy Path)
acceptance_summary.evidence: source_control.rs row() attaches menu via menu.show_on(&resp) with stable scm_row_{path_safe} author_id; scm_row_right_click_opens_menu_with_contract_items asserts six live leaves.

app_scm_stage_event_sends_real_post_on_the_wire: real TcpListener captures POST /source-control/stage and body {"repo_path":"/repo","paths":["src/x.rs"]}. Backend route verified real at source_control.rs:133 (post(stage)); handler accepts PathsRequest{repo_path,paths} (source_control.rs:172). apply_source_control_event (app.rs:3002) genuinely consumes source_control_client. Backend mounts routes with no /api prefix; client matches.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC#63: scm.diff_worktree calls GET diff?scope=worktree and updates diff state
acceptance_summary.evidence: app_scm_diff_event_sends_real_get_with_scope asserts GET /source-control/diff?...scope=worktree on the wire; backend DiffQuery.scope: DiffScope with #[serde(rename_all="snake_case")] deserializes 'worktree' correctly (source_control.rs:45-50); result drained into scm_display_text accessor.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC#8: Console row menu (Copy Line/Copy All/Filter by Kind submenu 5 children/Clear)
acceptance_summary.evidence: console_row_context_items + filter_submenu with 5 children (input/result/error/output/all); console_row_right_click_copy_line_copies_to_clipboard asserts leaves + filter_kind submenu header live.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC#65: console.copy_line copies row text to egui clipboard
acceptance_summary.evidence: console_row_right_click_copy_line_copies_to_clipboard reads OutputCommand::CopyText == 'run build' via harness.step() (single-frame retention, correct for transient clipboard command).
console_clear_empties_entries: 2 entries before, 0 after, on a persistent DebugConsole build_state harness.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC#11: Drawer item menu (Stow/Pin/Promote/Send to Pane/Copy to Prompt/Discard)
acceptance_summary.evidence: drawer_item_context_items with dynamic send_to_pane children under stable parent id drawer.send_to_pane; drawer_item_right_click_opens_menu_and_promote_dispatches asserts six leaves + Promote dispatch.

status_bar_context_items; status_bar_segment_right_click_opens_menu through REAL HandshakeApp (app.rs:4044-4058 renders the live segment, right-click opens, action applied) asserts four live leaves. This is the strongest surface — fully wired in production HandshakeApp::ui.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC#69: statusbar.open_panel on health opens System Status pane
acceptance_summary.evidence: status_bar_open_panel_opens_the_related_pane opens the REAL Problems pane. Independently confirmed PaneType has NO SystemStatus variant (pane_registry.rs:36-58) — the contract's content_type=SystemStatus is illustrative; statusbar_related_pane_type('health')->Problems (app.rs:588) is a disclosed deviation, label reads 'Open Problems'. The menu->pane wiring is proven live; intent (open the related panel) is satisfied.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC#70: all item ids exactly match stable ids via reference-slice unit test
acceptance_summary.evidence: loom_node/canvas_node/scm/console_row/drawer_item/status_bar _item_ids_match_reference tests (context_menu_surfaces.rs:1503-1860) compare collect_item_ids against exact contract reference vecs — verified non-tautological (each lists the literal contract id set).

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC#71: every right-click target has stable AccessKit author_id
acceptance_summary.evidence: scm_row_{path_safe} (source_control.rs:231), loom_node_{block_id}, canvas_node_{placement_id}, etc. set via accesskit_node_builder; menu items get ctx-menu.{id} from MT-019 infra; verified live in part2 author_nodes assertions.

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC#72: canvas.remove calls removeCanvasPlacement (real call asserted)
acceptance_summary.evidence: app_canvas_remove_sends_real_delete_placement asserts DELETE /workspaces/ws1/loom/canvas-placements/p9 on the wire; route verified real at loom.rs:344-347 (patch(update_canvas_placement).delete(remove_canvas_placement)).

acceptance_summary.criterion: AC#73: loom.pin toggles pinned and PATCH body contains {pinned:bool}
acceptance_summary.evidence: app_loom_pin_sends_real_patch_pinned_flag asserts PATCH /workspaces/ws1/loom/blocks/b3 body {"pinned":true}; set_flag_request builds exactly {"pinned":value} (backend_client.rs:221-224); backend patch_loom_block accepts pinned via LoomBlockPatchRequest{#[serde(flatten)] update: LoomBlockUpdate{pinned,favorite Option}} (loom.rs:1489, storage/loom.rs:340-343) — wire body deserializes correctly. Favorite analog ({"favorite":false}) tested too.

spec_realism.detail: Independently re-verified. No todo!/unimplemented!/panic!/unreachable!/not-yet-wired on any required-behavior path (grep clean across the seven touched src files). The real-resource claims (PostgreSQL-backed source-control + loom + canvas REST endpoints) are exercised against an in-process std::net::TcpListener capture server that asserts the EXACT HTTP method/path/JSON body the app's real clients emit through apply_source_control_event / apply_canvas_event / apply_loom_node_event handlers that genuinely consume the previously-dead clients off-thread on a real tokio runtime — not a self-authored mock. I independently confirmed the four backend route families EXIST (source_control.rs:132-133 GET diff + POST stage; loom.rs:88-93 block PATCH/DELETE; loom.rs:344-347 canvas-placements PATCH/DELETE; loom.rs:352-355 canvas-visual-edges DELETE) AND that the handlers accept the asserted wire shapes: PathsRequest{repo_path,paths}; DiffQuery.scope DiffScope snake_case ('worktree'/'staged'); LoomBlockPatchRequest flatten over LoomBlockUpdate{pinned,favorite} so {"pinned":true} deserializes with default empty add_tags/remove_tags; UpdatePlacementRequest all-Option-default so {"z_index":N} deserializes. The implementation correctly reconciled the contract's loose illustrative paths (/api/..., .../canvas/{cbid}/placements/...) to the actual route shapes. V1 stubs (loom connect/disconnect/delete, canvas connect_to/add_visual_edge/delete_block, scm discard/commit_this_file, drawer attach_evidence/convert_artifact) are rendered DISABLED+disclosed and map to None; scm.discard carries the STUB_NO_CONFIRM marker and scm_discard_is_disabled_and_does_not_fire proves it fires no event. Red-team controls honored: fresh-cached pin/favorite flip (loom_pin_favorite_reflect_fresh_state_and_flip), remove_edges DELETEs ONLY supplied visual-edge ids (app_canvas_remove_edges_deletes_only_supplied_visual_edges — never a semantic Loom edge), dynamic send_to_pane children under stable parent id. Spec-Realism sub-rule 3 (self-cert) is satisfied by THIS independent review per operator directive.

concerns_non_blocking: Structural divergence from contract 'EXPECTED DIFF SHAPE': the six menus are folded into context_menu_surfaces.rs + per-surface widget files rather than six separate files under src/frontend/context_menu/. Behavior is complete and exact-id-matched; only file layout differs. Topology/paperwork note, not a code FAIL (per operator directive).

AC#69 maps the health segment to the real Problems pane because WP-011 has no PaneType::SystemStatus (independently confirmed at pane_registry.rs:36-58); disclosed in code comments (app.rs:586-588) and the test, label reads 'Open Problems'. A dedicated SystemStatus pane is owned by a later pane-content WP.

Five of six surfaces (all but status bar) are not yet mounted as live pane-content factories; the widgets are fully driveable + proven standalone via egui_kittest, and the menu->event->client->HTTP path is proven live through the real HandshakeApp apply_*_event handlers. Final pane-host mounting is explicitly deferred to the owning pane-content WP — acceptable for this MT's scope.

status_bar 'Show {name}' restore label degrades to generic 'Show Segment' for the non-visible case because the menu model takes &'static str; the live right-click path is always the 'Hide' branch (a segment must be visible to be clicked). Restore is documented as a future settings-dialog (MT-018) dependency per the red-team control.

drawer send_to_pane child ids/labels and statusbar open_panel labels use Box::leak for dynamic &'static str; bounded by open-pane/segment count at right-click time — a small intentional per-interaction leak worth noting for a future intern-based id scheme.
